title: baccharis rufescens var. subrufescens
intro: Baccharis rufescens var. subrufescens, also known as Chilean tree or Baccharis, is a shrub native to South America, particularly in the Andean region. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Baccharis rufescens var. subrufescens include the ability to reduce fever, soothe respiratory issues, and alleviate digestive problems. The herbal preparations of Baccharis rufescens var. subrufescens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
